blocks.content: Thursday came back around the way Thursdays do — too slow until Monday and then suddenly it's already Wednesday night and you're still not ready.
I walked out after practice and she was there. On the steps. Same spot. A different book this time — thicker, worn at the spine.
She looked up before I reached the door. She always did that.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Good. I ran a post route without thinking about it first.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. That's the whole deal actually.
She smiled at that — the easy one, not the polite one. I was starting to know the difference.
I sat down. The number was right there in my head. Six digits and a plan. All I had to do was say it.
We talked for twenty minutes about nothing important. Then about something important. Then about nothing again.
